sn2.5:1.2: Then Dāmali the deva’s son, in the far extreme of the night, his extreme radiance lighting up the entirety of Jeta’s Grove, went to the Blessed One and, on arrival, having bowed down to him, stood to one side.
sn2.5:1.3: As he was standing there, he recited these verses in the Blessed One’s presence:
sn2.5:2.1: “This should be done by the brahman
sn2.5:2.4: he should not, through that, wish for becoming.”
sn2.5:3.4: As long as he hasn’t gained a footing in the river,
sn2.5:3.5: a man will strain with all his limbs.
sn2.5:3.6: But when he’s gained a footing
sn2.5:3.7: and is standing on firm ground,
sn2.5:4.1: he doesn’t strain, for he’s gone beyond.
sn2.5:4.2: This, Dāmali, is a simile for the brahman—effluents ended,
sn2.5:4.3: masterful, doing jhāna: Having reached the end of birth & death,
sn2.5:4.4: he doesn’t strain, for he’s gone beyond.”
